item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly I loved it except for making the woman a D class. Not just cruel for no reason but so much missed research potential!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don’t see any reason the woman would be made D Class, makes no sense. The foundation not in the habit of innocents being designated D class. She would also have valuable information about the scp as well as the life, culture, language, etc. of her era.
This detail (easily fixable), brought me right out of the idea of the scp.
Reluctant -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Way too useful, and while I don't mind useful Scips, the Foundation being needlessly cruel by making the woman a D-Class insted of just containing her is something I really did not like.
No vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My mother handed me the first one when I was 8. I was raised on that series!

Regarding the scip, I dunno. On the one hand, the story is a highlight. But as a whole, it feels too much like a stasis pod / hypnogourd.

Edit: first line was meant to convey excitement, not distain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now that you mention it, there is a certain similarity. I would argue it's superficial, however, and the story told in the endnote is worth an upvote.

Then again, I haven't read Xanth in over a decade. c.c

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Am I the only one who read this and thought of the Hypnogourds in the Xanth books? Sure, its minus the extra dimension, but it's a tad too on the nose for me.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tests seem out of order and needlessly sadistic. Tone issues. Woman shouldn't be made D class. Don't like this one bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Any human who touches the box with both hands and gazes at its bottom will instantly enter a state of stasis

What if they just look in without touching it? Or using gloves? Can a human see something at the bottom that a camera cannot?

I agree that this is a boring scp but the fun part is what they can do with it!
- A really cheap space station escape craft. Just grab a GPS transponder and exit the space station with or without a suit. A few days later, someone follows the GPS and picks you up!
- What's that SCP that kills people unless it's being stared at? Stick a SCP-1222-1 in front of that thing and save the Foundation some money in constantly getting new D-Classes.
- My preference is that the Foundation put this to use as unique safety equipment in their labs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall I like it, but I agree with, well, various people above that the woman shouldn't have been made D-Class. If nothing else, she represents a unique opportunity to study the effects of long-term 1222 exposure (which there may not be, but would any Foundation researcher worth their salt just assume that?), and any side effects from spending an extended period in stasis, plus her 19th century immune system, could complicate testing of other SCP items.

There's a difference between "containing anomalous persons" and "shoving every innocent we don't have an immediate use for into the D-Class barracks." I'd expect her to be given a unique sub-classification (SCP-1222-1-a or SCP-1222-2 or something) and a cell somewhere, so she can have regular medical checkups and interviews to make sure she's not going crazy or spontaneously re-entering stasis or anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I disliked that as well, but the rest of the article was too much fun. Plus, like dankaar said, she's from the 1800s—-it would be cruel and inhuman to just let her loose in a crazy future-world without anything but her raggedy-ass clothes.

Plus one thing I wish the article had gone into but didn't would be the likelihood of her catching and/or dying from modern-day diseases

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's Protocol-12 that basically states that the Foundation can do it if the need arises.

Anyway, the woman is a walking anomaly at this point; what else is the Foundation supposed to do? She was born in the 1800's: the world has likely changed to the point that she can't fit in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Down vote. I don't believe the Foundation abducts innocent people and makes D-class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Id about these other people but i love long test logs, the longer the better when it comes to testing logs. I lie it, upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find that the tone gets rather non-clinical at times, or could be phrased better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's just boring.

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Please take the time to read, rate and comment this article, it should be given more attention

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this really a cognitohazard (see tags)? For example, if you touch it while unconscious, does it still have the effect?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall this one is interesting, but why did they make the woman they found a D-class?
She seems like she would know a good bit about the object and she isn't a criminal so why would they kill her instead of making her a junior research assistant or something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, I get a little ahead of myself at times.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seems pretty interesting. One thing that bothers me though is that there isn't a description of what the bottom looks like, which is the most important part of the box. A robot should easily be able to take a picture of the bottom. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, at first I was like, "meh" … but the recovery log creeped me right the fuck out. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please refrain from using tags outside of those outlined in the Tag Guide and Attribute Tag Guide.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What did you mean by: "attempts to multiple successive instances"? Did you mean "multiply" or "create multiple successive instances"? You're missing a word, I think.

Other than that, upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Any human who touches the box with both hands and gazes at its bottom

I can't not read this as "Any human who touches the box with both hands and gazes at their bottom". So you grab the box and check out your ass and then stuff happens.

Misreadings aside, I don't find this terribly interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, although I do feel like the test log goes on much longer than it needs to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Any time I get the urge to go "Well this used to suck apparently but now it doesn't, I'mma go look to see how it used to be," I end up feeling bad for doing it.

I have not done it this time. Good on the original poster for an interesting concept, and good on Voct for making it palatable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it enough for an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed it, nice feel and not too much lolfoundation. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not doing anything for me, so no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the idea is ok and could have gone in any of several directions (passage to the future? preserve important figures who are getting old and have vital knowledge/experience, freeze someone whilst you sew them up/rig them to life support?), you basically went full lolfoundation but it's quite good lolfoundation and saves it imo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed it to the way I originally wanted it to appear, but had no idea on how to make collapsable boxes. I now do, so it's no longer a wall of text. Also Nuclear Materials has just been changed to Radiation. Because on a second thought, it was a stupid choice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Damn double post. If an admin reads this, please delete it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like it but i feel like the sheer amount of tests and future tests is a bit off-putting. I mean I know they want to make sure personnel are indestructable when holding it, but when you get to the point where you're nuking them, I think its a bit much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm upvoting this, because south-east Asian SCPs are neat, but I'd urge you to consider shortening this. It just seems too verbose for an idea that could be expressed quite simply.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Another Voct rewrite pulls through. You are a magician.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A bit of tone issues here and there, but otherwise pretty well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty nifty upvoted. Got two little nitpicks though…

First, you say that the stasis subjects can't be relocated, but then in two of the tests you state that they "fell over". So do you mean something specific by relocated that doesn't mean "can't be moved by any means"?

Second, I know they're blackboxed, but that's a LOT of SCPs that the researchers are supposedly considering testing this thing with. As a general rule I'm given to understand that cross-testing between SCPs are generally frowned upon…
(Take that second one with a grain of salt, I'm still new here and getting used to the dos and don'ts of the Foundation.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: S'aight

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much improved. I think the test logs are still somewhat long, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy crap Voct, that is fantastic. I'll go through when I am not so busy to correct any typos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Go right ahead. There was a typo in one of the tests I wanted to fix also.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's true, I thought it would be good for a little more back ground info. Too much I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would let Voct fix it, he has magic fingers (blessed by Satan Clef.)

Much better now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My mistake, I figured the Report Excerpt was a report excerpt and not a continuation of the description, my apologies.

Well your tone is lacking (that's normal) and you go into details that are completely unnecessary, for instance explaining that that it's a Lontar box and it's named after a palm tree (that kind of information doesn't matter to the Foundation.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's fixable. May I fix it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-1222 is safe when touched. Tests with D-Class personnel show SCP-1222’s effects do not take place until both hands of the test subject are touching the base and their eyes looking in toward the box. When this occurs, the test subject enters a dormant state of stasis. No breathing, blood pressure or brainwave functions are recorded. The test subject will remain in the position they were in when using SCP-1222, even when an outside force is pushed upon them. While the test subject is in this state it is possible for another to touch SCP-1222 without any changes."

What don't you care about in terms of the execution?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just read through it, you still don't tell us what it does in the description. Aside from that it's a pretty 'meh' concept and I don't care for the execution much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You should read it through first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You don't tell us what it does in the description, I'm not even sure what it does after I skimmed the rest of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was in the process of changing that. Wanted to first post the article before adding the picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"COPY-AND-PASTE-IMAGE-URL-HERE

TEXT-DESCRIBING-THE-PIC"

Come on, is it too much to ask that you preview even once before posting?
